popularStory.headline: Tucker Origins
popularStory.description:
by Garry Bryant

    For years all that existed on the Tucker lineage was the name of Ruth Tucker, wife of William Gilpin. Nothing else was known about the Tucker lineage. Patricia Brooks, a professio …

nameMeanings.name: Tucker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name from Middle English tuker(e), toker(e) ‘tucker, fuller’, a derivative of tuken ‘to torment, beat’ (Old English tūcian), for someone who fulled and finished cloth. This name for the occupation was characteristic of the West Country. Compare Fuller and Walker and see also Tuckerman .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tuachair ‘descendant of Tuachar’, a personal name composed of the elements tuath ‘people’ + car ‘dear, beloved’.
Americanized form of Jewish Tocker or Toker (see Tokarz ).

variants.name: Tooker
meanings: English (Devon) and Irish: variant of   Tucker  .

variants.name: Tuckerman
meanings: English: from Middle English tuker(e), toker(e) ‘tucker, fuller’ (see Tucker 1) + -man, either for a tucker, or for his servant or assistant.

variants.name: Fuller
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Swinger
meanings: English: occupational name, perhaps for a fuller, who finished cloth by beating it and stretching it on tenters. Middle English swyngere is not independently recorded but it appears to be an agent derivative of Middle English swingen ‘to beat, strike, punish, afflict (something)’. Compare the synonymous Tucker , an agent derivative of Middle English tuken ‘to torment, beat’. The earliest bearers of the name Swinger were wealthy townsmen.
In some instances probably an Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Schwinger , or German Zwinger, a nickname from Middle High German zwinger ‘oppressor’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Tucker was born in 1495, in Throwleigh, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Tucker Jr., was 26 and his mother, Dorothy Trecarell, was 25. He married Isota Josea Ashe in 1516, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1564, in his hometown, at the age of 69, and was buried in Dalwood, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Tucker est né en 1495, à Throwleigh, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Tucker Jr., avait 26 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Trecarell, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Isota Josea Ashe en 1516, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1564, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Dalwood, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat 1516 in Devon, England Isota Josea Ashe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Lui sposato Isota Josea Ashe nel 1516, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. ko.lifeSummary: William Tucker(이)가 1495년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 스로우리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Tucker Jr.(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Dorothy Trecarell(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1516년 영국 데폰에서 Isota Josea Ashe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1564년 그의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 다우드에 묻혔다.

ru.London St Edmund the King with St Nichol: лондон ст эдмунд т кинг вит ст ничолас акунс, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Tucker родился в 1495 г. в местности троулиг, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Tucker Jr. было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Trecarell, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Isota Josea Ashe в 1516 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1564 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности далвуд, девон, Англия, Великобритания.
